name: Laguna de Navaseca — circular desde Damiel
region_intro: Hiking around Daimiel offers a unique experience within the distinctive wetland ecosystem of Las Tablas de Daimiel National Park. This rare floodplain wetland, formed by the confluence of the Guadiana and Gigüela rivers, features a diverse hydrological environment. The landscape is characterized by a labyrinth of islands connected by wooden walkways, allowing access to tamarisk forests, common reed beds, and bulrushes. The region provides well-developed infrastructure for nature enthusiasts, primarily focused on observing its rich biodiversity. Best hiking trails around Daimiel The most popular hiking route is Isla del Pan Trail, a 1.8 miles (2.9 km) trail that takes 45 minutes to complete. This circular path features wooden walkways connecting several islands, ideal for observing diverse flora and fauna. Another top favourite among local hikers is Itinerario de la Torre de Prado Ancho (azul), an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route leads through four wildlife observatories before reaching a two-story tower offering panoramic wetland views. Local hikers also love the Las Tablas de Damiel — circular through the Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Damiel, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail leading through the heart of the national park, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Daimiel is defined by unique floodplain wetlands, wooden walkways, and diverse aquatic vegetation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Daimiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Daimiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Daimiel?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes in the Daimiel area. You'll find a good variety, with 17 easy trails, 7 moderate options, and 1 more challenging route, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What makes hiking in Daimiel unique?
A: Hiking in Daimiel offers a distinctive experience centered around the Las Tablas de Daimiel National Park, a rare floodplain wetland. This unique ecosystem, formed by the confluence of the Guadiana and Gigüela rivers, features a labyrinth of islands connected by wooden walkways, allowing you to explore diverse flora and fauna in an 'aquatic Mesopotamia' landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Daimiel?
A: The best season to visit Daimiel for hiking and wildlife observation is typically from April to June. During these months, the park is vibrant with activity, offering excellent opportunities to spot various bird species.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Daimiel?
A: Yes, Daimiel is very family-friendly, especially within Las Tablas de Daimiel National Park. Many trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373932611/isla-del-pan-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla del Pan Trail&lt;/a&gt;, feature mostly flat terrain and well-maintained wooden walkways, making them accessible for all ages and skill levels. There are 17 easy routes available in the area.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Daimiel?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Daimiel are circular, offering convenient loops for hikers. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201454493/las-tablas-de-damiel-circular-through-the-parque-nacional-de-las-tablas-de-damiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tablas de Damiel — circular through the Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Damiel&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the heart of the national park.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking in Daimiel?
A: Daimiel is a fantastic destination for wildlife spotting, particularly birds. Las Tablas de Daimiel National Park is a Special Protection Area for birds, home to over 200 species, including purple herons, widgeons, and black-necked grebes. You can also spot mammals like the red fox and European polecat. Many trails feature wildlife observatories for optimal viewing.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty of the National Park, you can explore several interesting points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/a&gt; itself is a major highlight. You might also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3694805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malvecinos Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Observation point&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. For cultural interest, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3817165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Antón (Carrión)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5292459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Encarnación&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers visiting Las Tablas de Daimiel National Park?
A: Yes, Las Tablas de Daimiel National Park provides parking facilities for visitors. The park is well-equipped for nature enthusiasts, and you'll find designated areas to park your vehicle before heading out on the trails.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Daimiel?
A: While the region is very nature-focused, specific regulations regarding dogs in Las Tablas de Daimiel National Park can vary. It's generally advisable to check the current park rules directly before bringing your dog, as national parks often have restrictions to protect wildlife.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Daimiel?
A: The hiking routes in Daimiel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique wetland landscapes, the abundance of wildlife, and the well-maintained wooden walkways that make the trails accessible and enjoyable for all.

Q: How long do the hiking routes in Daimiel typically take to complete?
A: Hiking durations in Daimiel vary depending on the route. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373932611/isla-del-pan-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla del Pan Trail&lt;/a&gt; is about 2.9 km and takes around 45 minutes. Longer routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201454493/las-tablas-de-damiel-circular-through-the-parque-nacional-de-las-tablas-de-damiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tablas de Damiel circular&lt;/a&gt; (8.4 km), can take over 2 hours. There are options for short strolls and longer excursions.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373944935/itinerario-de-la-torre-de-prado-ancho-azul-ida-y-vuelta-por-el-parque-nacional-de-las-tablas-de-daimiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itinerario de la Torre de Prado Ancho (azul)&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for panoramic views. This route leads to a two-story tower that offers impressive vistas of the wetland, particularly noted for birdwatching opportunities.
tips: About 2 kilometers from Daimiel, between the city and the Guadiana river following the road to El Nuevo, the Navaseca lagoon is part of the set of lagoons in the Daimiel district that were already described in the 16th century in the topographical reports ordered by Philip II.

The history of this 17th century church is closely linked to the Order of Discalced Carmelites first and, later, to the Discalced Carmelites, an order founded by Saint Teresa of Jesus.
Its façade reflects the traditional Carmelite scheme of the Baroque. Its sober and simple interior is structured in a Latin cross plan and a single nave with a transept and a dome on pendentives.

The Plaza de España in Daimiel is a beautiful example of a popular La Mancha square, with various image changes over the centuries, dating back to the 16th century, when it was known as Plaza de los Portales Blancos.
Since its origins it has been the nerve center of Daimiel, being during its history the place of celebration of different festivities, both bullfighting and cultural, as well as the space where markets were held.
Flanked by ten monumental pine trees, it is the main meeting, walk and leisure place. Currently, the Town Hall, several commercial establishments and financial entities are located in the Plaza.
